title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close today: Nominate a founder or submit your startup
summary: Today is the final day to apply or nominate a startup for Startup Battlefield 200. Once the clock strikes 11:59 p.m. PT, the window closes on your chance to compete for $100,000 in equity-free funding, gain global visibility, connect directly with investors, and launch on the TechCrunch Disrupt stage.

title: ElevenLabs’s new music generation model can switch genres mid-track
summary: ElevenLabs' new model will let users regenerate a section of a song without affecting rest of the track

title: Spotify now lets you ‘clip’ moments from your favorite podcast
summary: With the new scissors icon, you can clip favorite moments from podcasts and share them with your audience.

title: SOND, a sleep tech startup from Bose’s former head of sleep, exits stealth with $7M
summary: SOND, a startup led by Bose’s former head of sleep products, emerged from stealth with $7M in funding for its AI-powered sleep earbuds.

title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 Early Bird ticket savings end in 3 days
summary: There are only 3 days left to save up to $410 on your ticket to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Early Bird pricing ends May 29 at 11:59 p.m. PT, and once the deadline passes, ticket prices increase. If you plan to attend one of the most influential gatherings in tech this year, now is the time to lock in your pass before rates go up again.

title: China is increasingly keeping its best AI talent to itself
summary: China's AI boom is producing world-class talent, and Beijing is increasingly reluctant to let them go elsewhere.

title: ClickHouse triples anualized revenue to $250M, charting a path toward an IPO
summary: The database provider is eyeing a public debut within the next few years.

title: YouTube will now automatically label AI videos
summary: YouTube will now automatically label videos that use significant photorealistic AI, instead of relying solely on creators to disclose AI-generated content themselves. It's also making AI labels more prominent.

title: Tech CEOs are apparently suffering from AI psychosis
summary: "CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis," Box CEO Aaron Levie opines. Maybe that explains the almost religious belief in AI productivity gains.

title: Robinhood now lets your AI agents trade stocks
summary: Robinhood will let users create a separate account with pre-loaded balance that an agent use to trade

title: Airbnb-backed WeRoad raises $58M to take its group travel platform to the US
summary: WeRoad, the Milan-based group travel startup, has raised a $58 million Series C round led by Airbnb as it prepares for its first major expansion outside Europe. The funding brings the company’s total capital raised to roughly $100 million and will finance WeRoad’s push into the U.S., beginning with Austin. The new investment reflects a […]

title: UK Visa Portal spilled thousands of applicants’ passports and selfies online — and hasn’t fixed the leak
summary: The third-party website exposed applicants' sensitive documents as part of the U.K. visa application process. Instead of fixing the issue, the company sent attorneys.

title: What we’re looking for in Startup Battlefield 2026, and how to apply in time for the May 27 deadline
summary: Startup Battlefield applications are due tomorrow, so now's the time to put the finishing touches on your submission!

title: Trump administration permits Volvo to keep selling connected cars in the US
summary: Volvo, which is majority owned by China's Geely Holdings, said it can now move forward with its expansion plans for its U.S. factory.

title: Trump administration wants nuclear startups to use plutonium for their reactors
summary: The U.S. government is sitting on dozens of tons of weapons-grade plutonium. It's hoping startups can find a use for it.

title: DuckDuckGo installs are up 30% as users reject being ‘force-fed’ Google’s AI Search
summary: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026, replacing blue links with AI agents. The backlash has been swift. DuckDuckGo app installs spiked 30% as users seek a way out.

title: Starship’s path to reusability looks murky after SpaceX’s S-1
summary: SpaceX's recent IPO and Starship rocket test flight delivered two big data points that offer a realistic vision for the coming years — and one that may disappoint both the company's boosters and its critics.

title: SpaceX’s Starlink nabs American Airlines contract, another win for its IPO
summary: American Airlines said Tuesday it plans to install Starlink on more than 500 Airbus aircraft, the latest carrier win for IPO-bound SpaceX.

title: OpenRouter more than doubles valuation to $1.3B in a year
summary: OpenRouter has raised a $113 million Series B led by CapitalG. Its 5x growth in usage over six months indicates the multi-AI-model future is here.

title: This startup is betting India’s gig economy can train the world’s robots
summary: Human Archive, a startup founded by UC Berkeley and Stanford researchers, is paying gig workers in India to wear camera-equipped caps and sensor devices to collect the real-world physical training data that AI and robotics labs are racing to acquire.
